description: Expanded AI marketing and digital marketing knowledge pack for Marion. This file covers predictive analytics, recommendation systems, customer segmentation, attribution, content generation, media optimization, CRM personalization, lifecycle automation, experimentation, retail and e-commerce use, and governance constraints for AI-enabled marketing systems.

primary_text_references.title: Marketing Artificial Intelligence: AI, Marketing, and the Future of Business
Marketing Analytics: Data-Driven Techniques with Microsoft Excel
primary_text_references.author: Wayne L. Winston

primary_text_references.title: Digital Marketing Analytics: In Theory And In Practice
Marketing Data Science: Modeling Techniques in Predictive Analytics with R and Python

research_and_review_references.title: Machine learning and artificial intelligence use in marketing: a general taxonomy
How generative AI is shaping the future of marketing
The interplay of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and data analytics in digital marketing and promotions: A review and research agenda

case_study_references.title: Personalization at scale in consumer marketing
case_study_references.why_it_matters: Useful for understanding how AI and data infrastructure support individualized messaging, offer selection, and customer journey optimization.

case_study_references.title: Recommendation systems in digital commerce
case_study_references.why_it_matters: Shows how ranking, retrieval, and customer-behavior models affect product discovery, basket size, and retention.

case_study_references.title: Generative AI for marketing content operations
case_study_references.why_it_matters: Helps frame where generative AI assists with content velocity, localization, testing, and workflow compression without replacing measurement discipline.

case_study_references.title: Retail and e-commerce dynamic optimization
case_study_references.why_it_matters: Useful for connecting predictive modeling, demand signals, and promotional efficiency in commerce environments.

notes_for_marion: This pack should connect AI methods to actual marketing operating models rather than generic hype about automation.
Keep measurement central. Marketing claims are weak without incrementality, retention, and revenue linkage.
Treat personalization, targeting, and content generation as governance-sensitive areas because privacy, fairness, and deceptive-claims risk are always present.
Cross-link this pack later into ethics_law for privacy and consumer protection, and into psychology for persuasion, trust, and behavioral influence.

strengths: Valid JSON and coherent top-level object structure.
Commercially useful pack with metrics and governance kept in view.
Runtime path and registry-consumption metadata are now explicit.
issues: Bibliographic/source structures are present, but direct source URLs/DOIs/ISBNs are not attached in-file.
Direct source URLs/DOIs/ISBNs are still pending where not already present; no source identifiers were fabricated.
recommendedActions: Attach authoritative source URLs, DOI, ISBN, or publisher identifiers to reference entries.
Keep manifest and pack versions synchronized.
Use alias routing when layer labels differ from case-study domain labels.
Preserve bibliographic references without embedding copyrighted chapters.
criticalFixesApplied: added runtimeDefinitionIndex for direct AI definitions
added generic fallback suppression for known AI terms
added technical-debug regression block for definition prompts
normalized domain alias compatibility to runtime key ai
added ambiguityPolicy for overlapping AI subdomains
qualityAudit.remainingExternalDependency: Router must honor domain_question + knowledgeDomain=ai before technical_debug for definition prompts.

answerShape: one short explanation of AI-system relevance
clarifying branch only when the term is genuinely cross-domain
answerPolicy.technicalDebugPrecedenceRule: AI/domain definition prompts outrank technical_debug unless the user explicitly asks for autopsy, code, script, endpoint, patch, or validation.
answerPolicy.humanOversightBoundary: For AI governance, law, psychology, security, and high-impact decision support, keep human oversight and source limits visible.

ambiguityPolicy.template: Give a neutral definition, name the plausible AI subdomain interpretations, then ask which angle the user wants.

crossDomainBranches.ai_cybersecurity: security of AI systems, prompt injection, model/data integrity, and tool-use control
crossDomainBranches.ai_psychology: human cognition, trust, human-AI interaction, mental models, and affective computing
crossDomainBranches.ai_marketing: personalization, segmentation, recommendation, lifecycle automation, and measurement
crossDomainBranches.ethics_law: regulation, liability, privacy, IP, due process, and governance
crossDomainBranches.agents_systems: agent architecture, tools, memory, planning, orchestration, and evaluation
crossDomainBranches.foundations: core AI concepts such as learning, search, optimization, uncertainty, and evaluation

blockPhrases: I need one more concrete detail to route this cleanly.
The specific technical move is to name the active component
I’ll keep this broad and practical
Schedule depends on where you are.

runtimeDefinitionIndex.definition: AI marketing uses models and automation to improve segmentation, personalization, content operations, forecasting, attribution, and customer-lifecycle decisions.
Customer segmentation groups people by meaningful similarities such as behavior, value, needs, channel response, or lifecycle stage.
A recommendation system ranks or suggests items, content, offers, or actions based on user behavior, item features, context, or predicted relevance.
Attribution modeling estimates which channels, messages, or touchpoints contributed to a conversion or business outcome.
Customer lifetime value estimates the revenue or gross profit a customer may generate across the full relationship with the business.
Personalization adapts content, offers, timing, or experience to a user or segment while respecting consent, privacy, and fairness constraints.
Incrementality measures the lift caused by a campaign or intervention compared with what would likely have happened without it.
Predictive analytics is an AI concept in the ai marketing layer; answer it from this pack before using generic fallback.

crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.definition: Risk generally means exposure to possible harm, loss, uncertainty, or failure.
crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.clarifier: Do you mean security risk, legal/governance risk, business/marketing risk, or model-performance risk?

crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.definition: Validation means checking whether a model, system, claim, or workflow actually performs as intended under relevant conditions.
crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.clarifier: Do you mean model validation, security validation, legal/governance validation, or psychological measurement validation?

crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.definition: Alignment means making AI behavior match intended goals, user needs, policy boundaries, and human values.
crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.clarifier: Do you mean technical alignment, agent behavior alignment, governance alignment, or human trust/mental-model alignment?

crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.definition: Agency means the capacity to act toward goals; in AI, it usually refers to how much a system can plan, choose tools, and execute actions.
crossDomainAmbiguousTerms.clarifier: Do you mean agent autonomy, legal/ethical responsibility, or human perception of agency?
